hero.title: 3 Days in North Vietnam.
hero.titleAccent: Old Quarter Streets, UNESCO Limestone Karsts.
hero.intro: Vietnam's most iconic pairing in three days. Hanoi's ancient 36-guild streets, Hoan Kiem Lake, and Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum on day one — then a full day cruise through Halong Bay's 2,000 limestone karsts on day two. Sung Sot Cave, kayaking at Luon Cave, and climbing Titov Island for panoramic views of a UNESCO World Heritage landscape.

breadcrumb.label: 3 Days Hanoi & Halong Bay Super Saver

hero.heroImageAlt: Halong Bay limestone karsts UNESCO World Heritage Vietnam emerald water

overview.heading: 3 Days North Vietnam: Hanoi & Halong Bay Day Cruise
body: This three-day package pairs Hanoi's ancient streets with a full-day Halong Bay cruise — the two experiences that define North Vietnam for first-time visitors, packed into a compact and affordable format. You stay in Hanoi both nights, with day two dedicated entirely to the UNESCO World Heritage karst landscape of the bay.

Day one covers Hanoi's essential sights: Tran Quoc Pagoda (the oldest Buddhist pagoda in the city, on a peninsula in West Lake), Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum on Ba Dinh Square, Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple, St. Joseph Cathedral, the Hanoi Opera House, and the 36-guild Old Quarter. The city has been accumulating layers of history for over a thousand years, and the morning city tour with your guide provides the context that turns a list of landmarks into a legible story.

Day two transfers you to Tuan Chau Harbour on Halong Bay — a 2.5-hour drive through the Red River Delta lowlands. From the cruise vessel, you visit Sung Sot Cave (the largest and most dramatic cave in the bay), kayak through Luon Cave's enclosed lagoon, and climb halfway up Titov Island for a panoramic view across the karst landscape. A buffet lunch on board includes seafood and Vietnamese specialties. You return to Hanoi in the early evening.

highlights.heading: Why This 3-Day Vietnam Super Saver Stands Apart

items.title: Halong Bay — UNESCO World Heritage Karst Landscape
items.body: 2,000 limestone karsts and islands rising from emerald-green water in the Gulf of Tonkin — a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1994 for both its natural beauty and geological significance. The karsts have been shaped over 500 million years through tectonic movement and ocean erosion, creating a sea field of towers up to 200 metres high. Sung Sot Cave (Surprising Cave), the largest cavern in Halong Bay, contains chambers large enough to hold hundreds of people, with stalactites and stalacmites lit by coloured lighting that reveals the extraordinary scale of the interior.
items.imageAlt: Halong Bay UNESCO limestone karsts emerald water cruise boat Vietnam

items.title: Kayaking Through Luon Cave
items.body: Luon Cave is one of Halong Bay's most distinctive experiences — a sea tunnel through limestone karst that opens into a hidden enclosed lagoon surrounded by near-vertical rock walls. The kayak route through the cave is approximately 30 minutes: paddling through the dark tunnel into the flooded interior, where monkeys sometimes watch from the cliff ledges above. The lagoon on the other side is completely still, hidden from the open bay, and strikingly beautiful in the mid-afternoon light.
items.imageAlt: Halong Bay kayaking Luon Cave limestone karst lagoon Vietnam

items.title: Titov Island — Panoramic Bay Views
items.body: Titov Island, named after Soviet cosmonaut Gherman Titov who visited in 1962 with Ho Chi Minh, has a beach and a hillside with steps to a viewpoint roughly halfway up the island. The view from the upper steps — looking out across the karst field in all directions with nothing but limestone towers and green water — is the most complete panoramic view of Halong Bay available without a helicopter. The beach at the base is good for swimming.
items.imageAlt: Titov Island Halong Bay panoramic view limestone karsts Vietnam

items.title: Hanoi Old Quarter — 1,000 Years of Street Life
items.body: Hanoi's Old Quarter dates from the 11th century, when artisan guilds settled around the ancient citadel. Each street specialised in a single trade — silk, tin, paper, medicine — and the street names still reflect those origins today. The quarter is the most densely inhabited neighbourhood in Vietnam: motorbikes, colonial shophouses, street food vendors, and families living on lanes so narrow they never see direct sunlight. Hoan Kiem Lake, just south of the quarter, is where locals gather every morning and evening — Ngoc Son Temple sits on a small island connected to the shore by a red lacquer bridge.
items.imageAlt: Hanoi Old Quarter 36 guild streets colonial shophouses Vietnam

days.title: Arrive Hanoi — City Tour
days.description: Arrive at Noi Bai International Airport, met by your Dhesu guide at the arrival hall and transferred to your hotel. Hanoi city tour covering: Tran Quoc Pagoda (oldest Buddhist pagoda in Hanoi, on a peninsula in West Lake), Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum on Ba Dinh Square, Hanoi Old City Gate, Hoan Kiem Lake and The Huc Bridge (red lacquer bridge to Ngoc Son Temple), St. Joseph Cathedral, Hanoi Opera House, and the Hanoi Old Quarter (36 Ancient Streets). Sample local foods en route — pho, banh mi, and egg coffee (ca phe trung) are available within the Old Quarter. Evening free in the Old Quarter.
suggestedActivities: Noi Bai Airport arrival and hotel transfer
Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum and Ba Dinh Square
Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple

days.title: Halong Bay Full-Day Cruise — Sung Sot Cave, Kayaking & Titov Island
days.description: 07:30–08:00: Driver meets guests at hotel lobby for transfer to Tuan Chau Harbour via Hai Phong expressway (150 km, approximately 2.5 hours). Tea and coffee refreshment available at cruise check-in lounge while boarding is arranged. 10:00: Board cruise and begin voyage through Halong Bay World Heritage Site. Welcome drink on deck. The cruise passes landmark formations: Dog Rock, Duck Rock, and Thumb Rock (a limestone islet in the shape of a giant thumb). 12:00: Buffet lunch on board with seafood and Vietnamese cuisine. 13:00: Stop at Bo Hon Island to visit Sung Sot Cave (Surprising Cave) — the largest and most beautiful cave in Halong Bay, accessible via approximately 200 steps through forest. Explore the cave for about 45 minutes; those unable to walk may remain on sundeck. 14:00: Cruise to Luon Cave for 30 minutes kayaking through the sea cave and enclosed lagoon. 15:00: Stop at Titov Island — climb halfway up for panoramic views of Halong Bay. 16:00: Return to cruise for Sunset Party as the boat cruises through Hoa Cuong, described as one of the most beautiful areas of the bay. 17:30–18:00: Arrive back at Tuan Chau port; return transfer to Hanoi hotel.
suggestedActivities: Board cruise — welcome drink and bay views
Dog Rock, Duck Rock, Thumb Rock

days.title: Hanoi Free & Easy — Depart
days.description: Free morning in Hanoi for self-arranged activities. Options include: a final walk through the Old Quarter, a visit to Hoa Lo Prison Museum ('Hanoi Hilton'), the Vietnam Museum of Ethnology, or simply a slow breakfast at a Hoan Kiem café. Transfer to Noi Bai International Airport per your agreed schedule for departure.

whyBook.heading: Vietnam Made Easy — By the Agency Malaysians Have Trusted Since 1988
whyBook.body: Dhesu has been arranging Vietnam journeys for Malaysian travellers since 1988. Our guides in Hanoi know the city's history and the best street food spots. On Halong Bay, we work with cruise operators who maintain their vessels properly and time the bay visit for the best light. Every detail is arranged so you can focus on the experience.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience

sections.heading: 3 Day Hanoi and Halong Bay Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 3-day Hanoi and Halong Bay tour from Malaysia is the most efficient way to experience North Vietnam's two most iconic destinations. The package covers the Hanoi city highlights in a guided day tour — Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, Hoan Kiem Lake, the Old Quarter — and dedicates the full second day to Halong Bay. The day cruise format (return to Hanoi hotel in the evening) keeps the package compact and affordable while covering the essential Halong Bay experiences: Sung Sot Cave, kayaking at Luon Cave, and Titov Island viewpoint.

Malaysian passport holders can enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days, making this one of the most straightforward international destinations for Malaysian travellers. No advance visa application required.

sections.heading: Halong Bay Day Cruise vs Overnight Cruise
sections.body: This package uses a full-day cruise format: you board at Tuan Chau Harbour around 10am, spend approximately 7 hours on the water, and return to Hanoi by early evening. The day format covers Sung Sot Cave, Luon Cave kayaking, and Titov Island — the three most important Halong Bay experiences — in a single day. If you prefer to sleep on the water and experience the bay at dawn and dusk, our 4-day Hanoi and Halong Bay Luxury package includes an overnight cruise. Both formats let you see the essential karst landscape; the overnight adds the experience of the bay after dark and at first light.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders can enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. No advance application required — simply arrive at Noi Bai International Airport with your Malaysian passport and pass through immigration.

items.question: Is this package a day cruise or overnight cruise on Halong Bay?
items.answer: This is a full-day cruise. You board at Tuan Chau Harbour around 10am and return to Hanoi by early evening (around 8–9pm). You stay both nights in Hanoi. If you prefer an overnight cruise experience on the bay, our 4-day Hanoi and Halong Bay Luxury package includes one night on the water.

items.question: How far is Hanoi from Halong Bay?
items.answer: Approximately 150 kilometres by road, taking about 2.5 hours via the Hai Phong expressway. The transfer by private vehicle is included in the package. The road passes through the Red River Delta flatlands — a pleasant drive with good views of rural North Vietnam.

items.question: What is Sung Sot Cave?
items.answer: Sung Sot (Surprising) Cave is the largest cave in Halong Bay, discovered by French explorers in 1901 and given its name by visitors who were surprised by its scale. The cave is accessed via approximately 200 steps through forested hillside and contains two main chambers. The larger outer chamber is large enough to hold several hundred people, with vaulted ceilings and dramatic stalactite and stalagmite formations. The entire visit takes approximately 45 minutes.

items.question: What meals are included?
items.answer: Breakfast at the hotel daily (Days 2 and 3). On the Halong Bay cruise (Day 2), a seafood buffet lunch is included on board. Day 1 dinner and Day 2 dinner are at your own expense — your guide can recommend affordable and excellent restaurants near your hotel.
